commit.message:
Support reading secrets from files (#18)

* Initial plan

* Add support for reading secrets from files

- Update TypeBox schema to support either client_secret or client_secret_file
- Add normalizeConfig function to load secrets from files
- Update main.ts to validate and normalize config before use
- Update createApp.ts to use NormalizedConfig type
- Add comprehensive tests for config validation and file loading
- Update config.example.yaml to document new option

Co-authored-by: TastyPi <934844+TastyPi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review feedback

- Add comment explaining why index signature is needed in NormalizedClientConfig
- Use os.tmpdir() instead of hardcoded /tmp path in tests

Co-authored-by: TastyPi <934844+TastyPi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor tests and improve code documentation

- Add comment explaining error case in loadSecret function
- Refactor test cleanup using try/finally for better reliability
- Ensure test directory cleanup even on test failures

Co-authored-by: TastyPi <934844+TastyPi@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address PR feedback: use TypeScript's dual-use pattern for Config

- Remove ConfigType/ConfigSchema aliases, use Config directly
- TypeScript automatically uses Config as value or type based on context
- Add copilot-instructions.md to document this pattern
- Update README with configuration section explaining secret file support

commit.message:
fix: healing monitor artifact path regression from #692 (#706)

* fix: heal monitor artifact path — download to workspace root not test-results/

Artifact 'playwright-results-chromium' contains test-results/results.json
as its internal path. Downloading to path: test-results/ caused double
nesting (test-results/test-results/results.json), so healing-monitor.js
reported 0/126 tests passing and exited 1.

Fix: download to workspace root (path: .) so test-results/results.json
lands at the expected location. Added continue-on-error: true so the
monitor job doesn't hard-fail if the artifact is temporarily unavailable.

Regression introduced in #692 (matrix parallelisation).
Closes #704

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: npm audit fix — resolve basic-ftp CRLF injection vulnerability

GHSA-6v7q-wjvx-w8wg: basic-ftp <=5.2.1 high severity
Pre-existing vulnerability, not related to healing monitor fix.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add complete Grafana SQL query with SAI stat name mappings

grafana_query.sql contains:
- Main panel query with auto-downsampling, time range variables,
  and human-readable series names (e.g. Ethernet16_Port_IfInOctets)
- Variable queries for sai_type_id (shows 'Port' not '1')
  and sai_stat_id (shows 'IfInOctets (0)' not '0')
- 291 stat name mappings across 4 SAI object types:
  Port (204), Queue (47), BufferPool (25), IngressPriorityGroup (15)
- JOIN only on aggregated results (few thousand rows), not on raw data

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Fix subclass_selection_level from 2 to 3 (D&D 2024)

- Fix Abjurer: rename Improved Abjuration → Spell Breaker, update descriptions

revert: remove broken orthogonal self-loop code (#181, #182, #183) (#184)

Self-loops revert to bezier curves — the orthogonal self-loop
implementation had floating handles, broken hit testing, and made
selection impossible. The curved self-loop works reliably.

Non-self-loop orthogonal routing (arrows V2) is unaffected.

commit.message:
feat(phase3): Production Hardening & Sub-Agent Integration (#463)

* test(weakness+embedding+vector+redis): Sprint 13 coverage

- test_weakness_remediator.py: 37 tests covering all main flows, error handling, scoring, goal crafting
- test_embedding_provider.py: +16 tests for missing paths (_MissingPackage, edge cases, error handling)
- test_vector_store_v2.py: 17 tests covering VectorStoreV2 delegation to parent class
- test_redis_cache.py: +20 tests for error handling, lazy init, various data types

Coverage results:
- core/weakness_remediator.py: 99% (70/70 statements) ✅
- memory/embedding_provider.py: 100% (77/77 statements) ✅
- memory/vector_store_v2.py: 100% (10/10 statements) ✅
- memory/redis_cache_adapter.py: 93% (67/70 statements) ✅

All 118 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(neo4j+learning): Sprint 13 coverage

Add comprehensive test coverage for three memory modules:
- neo4j_bridge.py: 96% coverage (126/130 stmts)
- lesson_store.py: 100% coverage (41/41 stmts)
- learning_loop.py: 100% coverage (29/29 stmts)

Tests cover:
- Circuit breaker pattern (open, closed, half-open states)
- Neo4j driver initialization and connection handling
- Query and write operations with circuit breaker integration
- Codebase graph import functionality
- Brain synchronization
- Schema information retrieval
- File I/O and persistence
- Lesson derivation and injection
- Async record_cycle with lesson and debug report handling
- Edge cases: empty files, malformed JSON, empty lines

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test(cache-factory+momento+mcp): Sprint 13 coverage

- test_cache_adapter_factory.py: Complete coverage of factory functions
  * create_cache_adapter() with/without MOMENTO_API_KEY
  * get_adapter() for all types (local, momento, redis)
  * Error handling for invalid adapter types

- test_momento_adapter.py: Extended coverage to 77%
  * 20+ new tests for cache operations (get/set/delete)
  * Circuit breaker edge cases
  * List operations and publish functionality
  * Mock client integration tests

- test_mcp_cli.py: Added 50+ new tests for remaining gaps
  * HTTP client request handling and timeouts
  * Timeout configuration and env variable expansion
  * Tool listing, grep, and handler functions
  * Error handling and edge cases
  * All 239 tests passing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(blockers): add aura package root, error_resolution module, ast_analyzer timeout fix

- aura/__init__.py: missing package root caused all aura.* imports to fail
- aura/error_resolution/: new module required by existing tests
  - types.py: CacheKey, ResolutionConfidence, ResolutionResult dataclasses
  - safety.py: SafetyChecker — safe/sensitive/dangerous command classification
  - cache.py: LRUCache (L1) + SQLiteCache (L2, TTL) + FourLayerCache
  - engine.py: cache → known-fix → AI provider resolution pipeline
- agents/skills/ast_analyzer.py: replace recursive _max_nesting_depth with
  iterative stack implementation to avoid recursion limit / test timeout

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

SynthesizerAgent (15): goal/title, plan→intent, critique merging, file/test defaults
IngestAgent (22): _snapshot exclusions, run with/without ContextManager, hints parsing
MutatorAgent (30): path validation, ADD_FILE, REPLACE_IN_FILE, JSON mutations, edge cases
registry (48): FALLBACK_CAPABILITIES, _make_spec, _lazy_import caching,

  SandboxAdapter (dry_run, skip, pass/fail, snippet_count)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(prd-003): add LearningCoordinator, LearningArtifact types, learning loop — 106 tests

Port PRD-003 autonomous learning loop from claude/implement-ascm-v2-gSGye:
- core/learning_types.py: LearningArtifact dataclass (ARTIFACT_TYPES, SEVERITIES)
- core/learning_coordinator.py: LearningCoordinator with on_cycle_complete(),
  generate_backlog(), get_recent_artifacts(), _insight_to_goal()
- core/orchestrator.py + core/orchestrator_learn.py: wire coordinator into
  _record_cycle_outcome(); Phase 10 drains backlog into goal queue
- aura_cli/runtime_factory.py: instantiate + attach LearningCoordinator
- tests/test_learning_types.py: 33 tests for LearningArtifact
- tests/test_learning_coordinator.py: 34 tests for coordinator logic
- tests/test_learning_loop.py: 39 tests for CycleOutcome, QualitySnapshot,
  AdaptivePipeline, AutonomousDiscovery

Smoke test confirms artifacts=1 goals=[] for low-severity cycle learning.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs(prd-003): add design spec for LearningCoordinator + 106 tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(core+skills): cover GoalArchive, HumanGate, SkillBase, TypeCheckerSkill — 78 new tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(skills): cover CodeCloneDetector, TestCoverageAnalyzer — 47 new tests

* test(skills): cover LinterEnforcerSkill, _check_naming, _run_flake8 — 31 new tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(core): cover GitTools init, commit_all, rollback, stash — 16 new tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(memory): cover Brain remember/recall/tagged/set-get/weaknesses — 35 new tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(phase3): Complete Production Hardening & Sub-Agent Integration

This commit completes Phase 3 development with 173 new tests:

New Modules:
- aura/learning/ - Autonomous learning loop (feedback, patterns, optimization)
- aura/observability/ - Metrics and distributed tracing
- aura/semantic/ - Semantic context graph and relevance scoring
- core/subagent_integration.py - Sub-agent registry
- core/orchestrator_subagents.py - Orchestrator mixin

Phase 2 Sub-Agent Integration:
- IOTA: Error resolution → Error handling phase
- KAPPA: Recording → Workflow recording
- NU: Offline mode → Connectivity checks
- PI: Encryption → Config loading
- RHO: Health → Pre-flight validation
- SIGMA: Security → Security gate
- TAU: Scheduler → Background tasks

TUI Enhancements:
- observability_panel.py - Real-time metrics/traces
- health_panel.py - Component health status

Test Coverage:
- tests/aura/learning/ - 25 tests
- tests/aura/observability/ - 71 tests
- tests/aura/semantic/ - 25 tests
- tests/core/ - 49 sub-agent tests

Refs: Phase 3 milestone

commit.message:
fix: sanitize literal newlines in tool call JSON; enforce tool-result trust

Bug 1: sketch.js (and any multiline file content) silently not written
Root cause: models often embed raw newline bytes (0x0A) inside JSON string
values when the content is multiline code, producing invalid JSON that
json.Unmarshal rejects with 'invalid character newline in string literal'.
extractJSONObject found the boundary correctly but the Unmarshal step failed.

Fix:
- internal/orchestrator/parse.go: add sanitizeJSONControlChars() — a
  string-boundary-aware pass that replaces literal \n, \r, \t inside JSON
  string values with their proper escape sequences before Unmarshal
- Apply sanitizeJSONControlChars in both the single-line first pass and the
  block-scan second pass of parseToolCall
- Add two regression tests: LiteralNewlinesInContent and PrefixedWithProse

Bug 2: agent doubted tool output instead of trusting it
Root cause: no prompt instruction established tool results as ground truth;
the agent reasoned from its own (stale) history to dismiss the file listing.

Fix:
- internal/mcp/registry.go: add 'Tool results are ground truth' and 'tool
  call line must start with {' to the system prompt fragment injected into
  every agent's context
- internal/orchestrator/agent_loop.go: add same ground-truth principle to
  decomposePrompt and dmTurnPrompt; also add 'line must start with {' format
  reminder to prevent 'Lead plan: {...}' prose-prefixed tool calls

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
docs: comprehensive documentation update for v1.2.0

- Update source header to list all 30+ functions (was stale Phase 1 comment)
- Add Typed constructors section: msgpack_nil/true/false/bool, float32,
  int8/16/32, uint8/16/32/64, bin, ext
- Add Timestamp section: msgpack_timestamp, timestamp_s, timestamp_ns
- Add ext and timestamp rows to type system table
- Add timestamp row to JSON conversion mapping table
- Update msgpack_version() example to show 1.2.0
- Add MSGPACK_BUILD_BENCH and MSGPACK_BUILD_FUZZ to build options table
- Update spec compliance section with ext types and timestamp support
- Update loading section to mention typed constructors and schema validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Application Insights visitor tracking to all pages

- Create myaihealth-insights App Insights resource (Canada Central, workspace-based)
- Add JS SDK snippet to index.html, demo.html, terms.html, 404.html
- Cookies disabled (disableCookiesUsage: true) for privacy
- Update CSP in staticwebapp.config.json to allow js.monitor.azure.com,
  *.applicationinsights.azure.com, and *.monitor.azure.com
- Auto-tracks: pageviews, sessions, users, geography, referrers
- Cost: ~$0/month (free 5 GB/month ingestion tier)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add Docker publish and CLI release workflows

- server/Dockerfile: multi-stage Rust build (debian:bookworm-slim)
- web-ui/Dockerfile: multi-stage Node build (node:22-slim)
- server-publish.yml: build & push ghcr.io/yjgbg-labs/kanban-server on v* tag
- web-ui-publish.yml: build & push ghcr.io/yjgbg-labs/kanban-web on v* tag
- cli-release.yml: pkg cross-compile binaries + GitHub Release on v* tag

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
update

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: respect time period in Regions tab, add topN to Destinations, fix labels

- Territory map API now accepts period param and uses resolve_dates()
  for consistent date handling across all endpoints
- Regions tab passes period/startDate/endDate to API (was hardcoded YTD)
- Territory Map page also now respects time period selector
- Destinations tab: added 'Show top N' spinbutton control
- Renamed 'Total Revenue' → 'Total Bookings', 'Revenue by Region' →
  'Bookings by Region' to match terminology convention

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
